fact: March 23 sits exactly 82 days into the year, which means there are still 283 days left to go!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for maple trees to start dripping their sweet sap - some trees can produce up to 10 gallons per day!
Why do so many animals seem extra energetic right now? Because March 23 falls during the spring awakening when hibernating creatures are waking up and getting seriously hungry!
The sun rises and sets at almost exactly the same time everywhere on Earth around this date - thanks to the recent spring equinox making day and night nearly equal!
March 23 marks the transition from Pisces to Aries in the zodiac, meaning it's when the astrological year basically hits the reset button.
In Antarctica, this date signals the beginning of total darkness season - the sun won't rise again for months!
Earthworms go absolutely crazy around this time in temperate regions, coming up from deep soil as the ground thaws and becomes easier to wiggle through.
The moon phase on March 23 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it creates some of the most dramatic spring tides of the year.
In many northern cities, March 23 is statistically one of the last days you might see snow that actually sticks to the ground.
The jet stream - that invisible river of air high above us - tends to shift dramatically around this date, causing wild weather swings from winter to spring.
Frogs and toads emerge from their winter hiding spots around March 23 in many places, leading to the loudest nighttime choruses of the year.
